hero.desc: Keep the hair you still have with doctor-prescribed finasteride treatment for male pattern hair loss.

howItWorks.title: How Finasteride Works
howItWorks.desc: Understanding the science behind hair loss treatment
howItWorks.h1Desc: Finasteride inhibits 5-alpha reductase, reducing DHT levels by up to 70%
howItWorks.h2Desc: Less DHT means reduced miniaturization of sensitive hair follicles
howItWorks.h3Desc: Helps maintain existing hair and may stimulate regrowth over time

timeline.title: Your Treatment Timeline
timeline.desc: What to expect on your hair restoration journey
timeline.t1Desc: No visible changes yet. Your body is adjusting to the medication.
timeline.t2Desc: Reduced shedding and subtle improvements begin to appear.
timeline.t3Desc: Clear improvements visible. Best time to assess effectiveness.

fit.title: Is Finasteride Right For You?
fit.g1: Male pattern hair loss (temples, crown, mid scalp)
fit.g2: Early to moderate stages of thinning

safety.title: Side Effects & Safety
safety.desc: What you need to know before starting treatment
safety.se1Desc: Decreased libido, erectile dysfunction, and ejaculation disorders reported. Most resolve after stopping.
safety.se2Desc: Monitor for depression or anxiety. Stop immediately if suicidal thoughts occur and contact your doctor.
safety.se3Desc: Finasteride lowers PSA levels. Inform your doctor before any PSA tests.
safety.se4Desc: Seek medical advice if you notice lumps, pain, swelling, or nipple discharge.
safety.noteText: Most men take finasteride without major issues. Side effects are possible but often reversible. The key is awareness and early action. Discuss your full medical history, including any history of depression or sexual health concerns, during your consultation.

cost.title: Transparent Pricing
cost.priceNote: *Prices are estimates and may vary based on your specific treatment plan

faq.q1: Does finasteride regrow hair or just stop hair loss?
faq.a1: It can do both. Many men see slower hair loss, and some experience new growth. Results vary by stage and consistency of use.
faq.q2: How long does finasteride take to work?
faq.a2: Most people need 3 to 6 months to notice improvement. A full assessment typically requires closer to 12 months of consistent use.
faq.q3: What happens if I stop taking finasteride?
faq.a3: The benefits gradually fade over time, and hair loss usually resumes. Finasteride only works while you're taking it consistently.
faq.q4: Can finasteride cause depression or anxiety?
faq.a4: Mood changes have been reported. Regulators advise vigilance and to stop finasteride 1mg for hair loss if depression or suicidal thoughts occur.
faq.q5: Is finasteride safe for long-term use?
faq.a5: Many people use it for months or years. Long-term use should include regular monitoring for side effects with your healthcare provider.

cta.title: Ready to Start Your Hair Restoration Journey?
cta.desc: Don't wait until thinning becomes obvious. A short medical consult can tell you what type of hair loss you have and whether finasteride makes sense for you.
cta.address: Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, KLCC area, Kuala Lumpur
